Director/PDMR Dealing
The Board of Naked Wines announces that Eschler Global Fund SPC - Colebrooke Opportunities Fund SP, a person closely associated with Jack Pailing, Non-Executive Chair of the Company, has today purchased 38,000 ordinary shares of 7.5 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares").
Following the purchase of shares, Mr. Pailing's beneficial interest in the Company and that of persons closely associated with him is 1,011,843 Ordinary Shares representing 1.50% of the total voting rights of the Company.

About Naked Wines
Naked Wines
is not just an online wine retailer; we're trailblazers on a mission to enable enthusiastic wine drinkers to enjoy great wine without the guesswork.Founded in 2008, on the pillars of quality, choice and fair pricing, we set out to create the most inclusive wine club in the world - dedicated to transforming the wine-buying experience and empowering people to make their own wine choices, and championing world-class independent winemakers. We've proudly been delivering outstanding wines to our customers (who we call Angels) for over 16 years.
Our business model is simple yet innovative: Naked Wines funds the production costs for winemakers upfront, allowing them to focus on creating exceptional wines without the financial burdens of traditional wine production, while passing the resulting savings back to our customers.
The virtuous circle is a win-win for both wine lovers and winemakers, and enables us to deliver superior benefits to our customers:
- Better quality wine
- More choice
- Personalised wine recommendations
- Elimination of guesswork and uncertainty
- Fair payments for all involved
Our Angel customers in the UK, US and Australia have direct access to over 300 world-class independent winemakers and over 2,500 quality wines from 23 countries.
For more information visit
nakedwinesplc.co.uk
and
nakedwines.co.uk
or find us
@nakedwines

latest.title: Transaction in Own Shares; Completion of Buyback
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4912W
Naked Wines PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Naked Wines plc
('Naked Wines' or 'Group')
Transaction in Own Shares and Completion of Share Buyback Programme
Naked Wines announces that, in accordance with the terms of its share buyback programme announced on 4 February 2026 ("
Share Buyback Programme
"), the Company purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of 7.5 pence each ("
Ordinary Shares
") through Panmure Liberum Limited.
Date of purchase:
12/03/2026
Aggregate number of Ordinary Shares purchased:
215,000
Lowest price paid per share (GBp):
72.75
Highest price paid per share (GBp):
72.75
Volume weighted average price paid per share (GBp):
72.75
Following the purchase of these shares, the remaining number of Ordinary Shares in issue will be 71,717,079 with 4,105,665 shares held in treasury. The total voting rights in the Company will therefore be 67,611,414. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), as it forms part of Retained EU Law as defined in the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, the table below contains detailed information of the individual trades made by Panmure Liberum Limited as part of the Share Buyback Programme.

Venue

72.75
11:17:16
00044038363TRLO0
AIMX
Following the above purchase of shares, the Company announces that it has now completed its Share Buyback Programme, to acquire up to a maximum aggregate consideration of £1 million.
In total, the Group repurchased 1,339,000 ordinary shares at an average price of 74.60 pence, for a total consideration of £998,996.

company_30s.what_they_do: WINE.L produces and sells premium wines for retail and hospitality customers.  Its product line includes a range of reds, whites and sparkling options, many of which are bottled under its own brand.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global wine market is worth billions of pounds and is growing as consumers look for higher‑quality, boutique options.  WINE.L has a focused brand that can capture a share of the premium segment, giving it a clear niche against larger, commodity‑oriented producers.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently launched a share‑buyback program and is in the process of returning capital to shareholders.  Management is also exercising share options, indicating confidence in the business.  The stock is trading far below its recent highs, suggesting it is undervalued relative to its own valuation.

rns_narrative: The company started a buyback program in early February and has now reached about 85 % of its target, showing strong capital‑return activity.
Recent RNS releases show directors buying and selling shares, and the granting of new share options, signalling that insiders are backing the company.
There are no major negative announcements – no delisting, no bankruptcy filings – so the focus is on the buyback as a potential catalyst.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the rapid buyback progress as evidence that management believes the shares are undervalued and that the stock will rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears note that the price is still far below the 52‑week high and that the company’s earnings history is thin, so further downside is possible if the buyback stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has been largely silent, but the few posts that exist are bullish, driven by the buyback updates.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning there is no current public interest – a perfect setup for a surprise rebound.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the buyback program, which could trigger a price lift as the company’s equity base shrinks.
A potential dividend announcement or a positive earnings update that would confirm the company’s financial health.
A noticeable spike in trading volume that would show renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a breakout above the recent resistance level and a volume surge of at least ten thousand shares.  A clear move above the 0.02 support zone would be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in a late‑stage washout.  With a tight compression pattern, the APEX score suggests the next move could be a sharp rebound if the buyback momentum continues.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep drop has created a large upside potential – a move back to the all‑time high would be roughly four times the current price.  The buyback program is a strong sign that management is willing to support the share price, and insiders are buying more stock, which further backs the thesis.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The price remains far below its recent high and the company’s earnings track record is modest.  If the buyback stalls or if the market stays indifferent, the stock could fall further, especially if volatility spikes.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose the current price level but stand to gain fourfold if the stock reaches its high.  The risk/reward ratio is roughly 1:4 – an attractive asymmetry for a contrarian trade.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a clear breakout of the 0.02 resistance zone, place a stop 0.01 below the entry, and size the position to about 10 % of your portfolio.  Keep an eye on volume; a spike will confirm the move.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume increase of at least ten thousand shares and a price above 0.02 before committing.  If the breakout fails, consider tightening the stop to protect capital.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals, so the trade remains viable.  Only consider exiting if the buyback stalls or if the price breaks back below the 0.01 support with heavy selling.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash and a fast‑moving buyback program, WINE.L offers a contrarian play that could deliver up to four times the current price if the market reacts to the capital‑return catalyst.
